title: Meniscium nesioticum
intro: Meniscium nesioticum is a species of flowering plant in the family Polypodiaceae, commonly known as a type of fern. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, which can aid in wound healing and reduce the risk of infections. The bioactive constituents of Meniscium nesioticum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and extracts, often in combination with other herbs, to support overall health and well-being.
taxonomy: Meniscium nesioticum is classified as a species of fern in the family Thelypteridaceae. This family consists of over 1,000 species of ferns, commonly known as the marsh ferns or the shield ferns. Meniscium nesioticum is a type of fern that is native to tropical regions. It belongs to the genus Meniscium, which is a group of ferns that are known for their unique shield-shaped fronds. The classification of Meniscium nesioticum places it within the kingdom Plantae, phylum Pteridophyta, class Polypodiopsida, and order Polypodiales.
